title: Rejected promise cached permanently, poisoning all subsequent loadFixture calls for that id
description: cache.set(id, p) is called before p resolves. If the inner async block throws (e.g. ENOENT on a missing fixture file), the rejected Promise is stored in cache. Every subsequent call to loadFixture(id) retrieves the cached rejection and re-throws without re-attempting the load. A transient error (missing file that is later generated by generate:fixtures) becomes permanent for the lifetime of the module, requiring a process restart to clear.

title: pickPgoSourceFiles limit defaults to DEFAULT_SOURCES.length (3) regardless of the sources argument
description: The exported function signature is `pickPgoSourceFiles({ sources = DEFAULT_SOURCES, limit = DEFAULT_SOURCES.length } = {})`. When callers pass a custom sources array with more than 3 entries, the default limit of 3 silently truncates them. The limit default should be `sources.length` (evaluated after sources is bound), but parameter default expressions cannot reference sibling parameters in JS. This means callers who pass 5+ sources expecting all to be returned will silently get only 3.

title: Division by (width-1) and (height-1) produces NaN when width or height is 1
description: All render functions (renderSrgb8, renderSrgbAlpha8, etc.) compute gradients via `x / (width - 1)` and `y / (height - 1)`. When width or height equals 1 the divisor is 0, producing NaN, which rounds to 0 via Math.round. Hardcoded 100x100 dimensions prevent this today, but the functions are generic and not guarded. If width=1 or height=1 is ever passed (e.g. for a tiny regression fixture), all gradient pixels silently become 0 instead of a divide-by-zero error.

title: saturated-green-16bit claims colorSpace 'adobe-rgb' but has no ICC profile (hasIcc: false)
description: The fixture is declared with `colorSpace: 'adobe-rgb'` but `hasIcc: false`. An Adobe RGB image without an embedded ICC profile cannot be distinguished from sRGB by decoders — libjxl will decode it as sRGB by default. Any test consuming this fixture and asserting wide-gamut color behaviour will get silently incorrect results. The fixture was rendered as a pure linear green ramp with no color-space encoding; the colorSpace metadata should be 'srgb' or the ICC profile should be embedded.
code_excerpt:
id: 'saturated-green-16bit',
      filename: 'saturated-green-16bit.jxl',
      ...
      colorSpace: 'adobe-rgb',
      hasAlpha: false,
      hasIcc: false,

title: lossless-16bit claims colorSpace 'adobe-rgb' but has no ICC profile (hasIcc: false)
description: Same issue as saturated-green-16bit: `colorSpace: 'adobe-rgb'` with `hasIcc: false`. The fixture was rendered as a generic 16-bit RGBA ramp with no color-space metadata embedded in the JXL file (encode options show `iccProfile: null`). The colorSpace field misrepresents what was actually encoded, which could cause tests relying on wide-gamut behavior to produce false passes or false failures.
code_excerpt:
id: 'lossless-16bit',
      filename: 'lossless-16bit.jxl',
      ...
      colorSpace: 'adobe-rgb',
      hasAlpha: false,
      hasIcc: false,

title: progressive-dc-truncated truncation is whichever is SMALLER, potentially yielding fewer bytes than the DC layer
description: The comment says 'whichever is smaller' and the intent is to keep only the DC layer. The expression `Math.min(400, Math.floor(progressiveJxl.byteLength * 0.35))` means if the file is ~1200 bytes, truncSize = min(400, 420) = 400, which may land AFTER the DC passthrough boundary. If the file is very small (< ~1143 bytes), the 35% heuristic dominates and may truncate before a usable DC frame is available. There is no validation that the truncated bytes actually contain a complete DC frame; a test that expects a partial progressive decode from this fixture may silently receive no frames.

title: No startup invariant check that manifest colorSpace/hasIcc/hasExif metadata is consistent with what was actually encoded
description: Manifest metadata fields (colorSpace, hasIcc, hasExif, hasXmp) are hand-authored and can drift from what generate-fixtures.mjs actually encodes. Currently two fixtures have incorrect colorSpace values (logic-4, logic-5). A small validation pass at the end of generate-fixtures.mjs — or a separate verify-manifest script — that decodes each fixture and checks that the declared metadata matches the actual libjxl-reported values would catch these mismatches automatically at generation time.

category: no property-based testing on math/state-heavy code
title: downscaleRgba has no unit tests; area-average weight accumulation can silently produce black pixels
description: The in-process downscaler computes fractional pixel weights for area-averaging. If `wSum` rounds to exactly 0 due to floating-point precision at boundary pixels (e.g. when sxMin == sxMax after flooring/ceiling), the destination pixel is left as 0 (black) with no warning. Additionally the inner loop bounds `sy < Math.ceil(syMax)` paired with the clamp `if (sy >= height) continue` is correct, but there are no tests exercising edge cases: 1-pixel wide source, scale < 0.5, integer scale factors. A property test asserting sum(dst) ≈ sum(src) * scale² would catch accumulation bugs.
